encounter.reason: annual wellness examination, cervical cancer screening, COVID-19 vaccination
encounter.location: Hawthorne Health Center, 3847 SE Hawthorne Blvd, Portland, OR 97214

conditions.name: bipolar II disorder
conditions.note: Stable on current regimen of lamotrigine 200 mg daily and quetiapine 50 mg nightly, managed by psychiatry (Dr. Okafor). PHQ-2 negative. No changes from PCP perspective.

conditions.name: major depressive disorder
conditions.note: Historical, initially diagnosed 2019, subsequently reclassified under bipolar II diagnosis. Documented as resolved.

medications.name: lamotrigine 200 mg oral tablet, once daily
medications.sig: lamotrigine 200 mg by mouth once daily

medications.name: quetiapine 50 mg oral tablet, once daily at bedtime
medications.sig: quetiapine 50 mg by mouth at bedtime

immunizations.name: COVID-19 vaccine, mRNA (Pfizer-BioNTech), dose 1
immunizations.note: lot number documented; second dose scheduled for April 2021

immunizations.name: Tdap (tetanus, diphtheria, and acellular pertussis) vaccine, booster
immunizations.note: last dose in adolescence, overdue per adult schedule

document_references.description: Documents the annual wellness visit for a 27-year-old female including review of systems, physical examination, vital signs, PHQ-2 depression screening (negative), cervical Pap smear collection, administration of influenza, COVID-19 dose 1, and Tdap immunizations, annual lab orders, medication reconciliation of psychiatric medications, allergy confirmation, and follow-up plan for one year.

diagnostic_reports.description: CBC ordered at annual wellness visit; results reviewed at a later date and reported as within normal limits. No individual component values provided.
CMP ordered at annual wellness visit; results reviewed at a later date and reported as within normal limits. No individual component values provided.
Cervical Pap smear collected at visit for routine cervical cancer screening. Results returned approximately two weeks later as negative for intraepithelial lesion or malignancy (NILM).
